channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: AI-generated images can be art. They just can’t be photos
item.description: As a kid of the 1970s, I was fascinated by a short-lived art movement known as photorealism. The painters who practiced it created works that weren’t merely realistic. They were borderline indistinguishable from photographs—an extraordinary feat to pull off with oil on canvas.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Prince fans will finally get to hear the Purple Rain in Dolby Atmos, as the classic film comes to Dolby Cinemas for a one-night-only screening
item.description: The film Purple Rain will be re-released with better visuals and audio for a one-night-only event at Dolby Cinemas across the United States and the United Kingdom.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: OpenAI cofounder Ilya Sutskever’s new AI startup is fundraising with a $30 billion valuation
item.description: A new artificial intelligence company from one of the cofounders of OpenAI is quickly becoming one of the most highly valued AI firms in an increasingly crowded marketplace.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Need a bodyguard? Now there’s an app for that
item.description: A new app makes hiring security as simple as ordering an Uber. A viral TikTok with 11.2 million views reads, “POV we just ordered bodyguards to pick us up from the airport.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: OVHcloud US: Addressing businesses’ complete cloud needs
item.description: Based in Reston, Virginia, OVHcloud US is a wholly owned subsidiary of OVH Cloud, Europe’s leading cloud provider.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: An obscure French startup just launched the cheapest true 5K monitor in the world right now and I can't wait to test it
item.description: JapanNext challenges the market with an affordable 5K monitor, delivering high resolution and color accuracy at a low price point.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: AI-created disinformation could bring down banks
item.description: A new report by UK analyst firm Say No to Disinfo and communications firm Fenimore Harper indicates a high risk that AI-generated disinformation could create bank runs that could bring down financial institutions, according to Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: No, it's not an April fool, Intel debuts open source AI offering that gauges a text's politeness level
item.description: Intel launches Polite Guard, an AI model that ensures digital interactions remain polite, respectful, and contextually appropriate.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: EXL Code Harbor streamlines platform migration, data governance, and workflow assessment
item.description: With their outdated technology and high costs, legacy codebases hold enterprises back. But in many cases, the prospect of migrating to modern cloud native, open source languages1 seems even worse. Migration projects typically require significant time and money.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Sea turtles return to the Atlantic Ocean off Florida after being stunned by the cold
item.description: Eight green sea turtles stunned by the cold during a rare snowfall in Florida have been released back into the Atlantic Ocean.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How to maximize hard-dollar ROI on security technology investments
item.description: Fresh research from PWC has found that CIOs are far less likely to say that the IT function is prepared for reinvention, compared to 2023. The drop is considerable: 20 percentage points in just 12 months indicates a significant loss of confidence that IT is ready to modernize.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Fancy a drone destroyer? Someone transformed a Framework 16 laptop into a UAV killer thanks to a custom SDR module
item.description: A hardware designer has developed a drone jammer using a custom SDR module for the Framework modular laptop.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Bay Area job market sickened due to COVID has yet to recover
item.description: The Bay Area job market, an engine for California's economy, has sputtered badly in the wake of the coronavirus outbreak.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: DOGE and Musk: The 5 biggest controversies so far
item.description: Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) has caused uproar almost from the moment of its inception. DOGE is not an official government department, despite its name. Only Congress can create new departments.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, The Hill

item.title: Mistral turns focus toward regional LLMs with Saba release
item.description: French AI startup Mistral is turning its focus toward providing large language models (LLMs) that understand regional languages and their parlance as a result of rising demand among its enterprise customers. “Making AI ubiquitous requires addressing every culture and language.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Serverless was never a cure-all
item.description: An obituary for serverless computing might read something like this: In loving memory of serverless computing, born from the fervent hopes of developers and cloud architects alike.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 3 reasons to consider a data security posture management platform
item.description: A week rarely goes by without a major data security breach. Recent news includes a breach impacting an energy company’s 8 million customers, another compromising the information on 450,000 current and former students, and one more exposing 240,000 credit union members.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Neudesic seeks to speed up AI adoption for IT teams
item.description: Artificial intelligence (AI) has gained significant traction among business leaders keen to explore ways it can drive operational efficiencies and cost savings. But while top leadership is sold on its potential, it’s a different tale for IT teams working the ground.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How DoD Modernization Can Advance Mission Readiness for the Warfighter
item.description: Discover how the Department of Defense's modernization efforts are transforming the way it supports its warfighters, with a focus on innovative business processing and technology solutions.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Roberts, Federal News Network

item.title: Court ban on Google AI stakes would hurt Anthropic clients, say analysts
item.description: Anthropic has asked a US court for permission to intervene in the remedy phase of an antitrust case against Google, arguing that the US government’s call for a ban on Google investing in AI developers could hurt it.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: GenAI can make us dumber
item.description: Generative AI (genAI) tools based on deep learning are quickly gaining adoption, but their use is raising concerns about how they affect human thought.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Net neutrality under Trump? Not so neutral
item.description: Even before President Donald J. Trump returned to office last month, net neutrality took a punch to the jaw. On Jan. 2, the US Court of Appeals for the Sixth Circuit struck down the Federal Communications Commission’s (FCC) net neutrality rules. Oh well, it was nice while it lasted.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth and consumers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Even as he's vowed to push the United States ahead in artificial intelligence research, President Donald Trump's threats to alter federal government contracts with chipmakers and slap new tariffs on the semiconductor industry may put new speed bumps in front of the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What Elon Musk did before DOGE
item.description: Elon Musk may be the face of DOGE, but he made a name for himself in businesses built around technology and space travel.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Autopsy: OpenAI whistleblower Suchir Balaji died of suicide
item.description: The cause of Suchir Balaji's death -- released on Friday afternoon by the San Francisco County Medical Examiner's Office -- ran counter to claims Balaji's family has aired in recent months that questioned the circumstances of his death.
item.dc:creator: Jakob Rodgers, The Mercury News

item.title: OpenAI board unanimously rejects Elon Musk's $97.4 billion proposal
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — OpenAI says its board of directors has unanimously rejected a $97.4 billion takeover bid by Elon Musk. “OpenAI is not for sale, and the board has unanimously rejected Mr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban. TikTok, which is operated by Chinese technology firm ByteDance, was removed from Apple and Google’s app stores on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Palo Alto Networks firewall bug being exploited by threat actors: Report
item.description: Admins with firewalls from Palo Alto Networks should make sure the devices are fully patched and the management interface blocked from open internet access after the discovery this week of a zero-day login authentication bypass in the PAN-OS operating system.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: China launches center to train 100-plus humanoid robots simultaneously
item.description: A humanoid robot training center has opened in Shanghai, a big step in China's efforts to become a global leader in robotics and artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Scout Motors' effort to directly sell its electric SUVs where they'll make them stalls
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Legislation to let Volkswagen-backed Scout Motors sell its electric SUVs directly to people in South Carolina, where the vehicles will be built, has stalled after fierce opposition from traditional automobile dealers.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Diving into the Windows Copilot Runtime
item.description: Announced at the May 2024 launch of Arm-powered Copilot+ PCs, the Windows Copilot Runtime is at the heart of Microsoft’s push to bring AI inferencing out from Azure and on to the edge and our laptops.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Go 1.24 arrives with generic type aliases, boosted WebAssembly support
item.description: Go 1.24, an update to Google‘s popular open source programming language, is now generally available as a production release, with full backing for generic type aliases, performance improvements, and improved WebAssembly support. The release was unveiled February 11, and can be downloaded from Go.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Tech security 101: How to spot a hidden tracker on your car
item.description: 80% of stalking victims are tracked using tech. GPS trackers, like AirTags, are among the most popular ways to do it. Here's how to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thomson Reuters scores early win in AI copyright battles in the US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Thomson Reuters has won an early battle in court over the question of fair use in artificial intelligence-related copyright cases.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: VP calls for ideology-free AI
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
